channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Four Teams Remain at CS Asia Championships After Bumper Friday
item.content:encoded:
The CS Asia Championships 2026 saw both the Group Stage conclude and its Quarter-Finals contests on a bumper Friday, which left only four teams in the competition.

Falcons, MOUZ, MIBR, and proceed to CS Asia Championships Semis

The CS Asia Championships 2026 will open up the arena to fans at the weekend as the tournament progresses to the Semi-Finals stage.

Falcons and MIBR advanced directly to the semi-finals after Group Stage Upper Final wins against Legacy and B8, respectively.

Falcons had one of their cleanest wins so far with the roster, blowing Legacy out of the water 13-7, 13-9. Considering Legacy defeated NAVI last week at IEM Atlanta, the victory was a positive sign for Finn ‘karrigan’ Andersen and his men.

For MIBR, a Semi-Final berth came in a more labored sense, but arguably more decisively. It took three maps, but they only lost three rounds in total on the maps they won, inspired by a rabid Felipe ‘insani’ Yuji who blew B8 away.

MOUZ then joined the Semi-Finalists with a 2-0 Quarter-Final victory over B8. The roster lost their opening contest against TYLOO at CAC, but has not looked back since, taking down B8 13-8 and 22-20 after a barnstorming Ancient contest.

Lastly, Legacy booked their place in a chaotic Bo3, which was decided by the second-last round of regulation on the third map of Nuke, in a 1v3 clutch from Bruno ‘latto’ Rebelatto.

The series perfectly encapsulated why The MongolZ and Legacy are so entertaining to watch, but also highlighted the frailties of their styles that make things like that 1v3 possible.

Ultimately, Legacy got the better of The MongolZ, who will be disappointed at their sloppy exit of a rare Tier 1 tournament in thier home region.

The CS Asia Championships semi-finals are:

  • Falcons vs MOUZ
  • MIBR vs Legacy

The winners will progress to Sunday’s Best of 5 Grand Final, with the losers contesting a Third/Fourth place decider.

Parivision, paiN, B8, and The MongolZ eliminated in Shanghai

paiN were the first team to fall out of the competition on Friday, losing 2-0 to this Beta test version of a MOUZ roster. The Brazilian roster failed to back up their IEM Atlanta playoff berth, losing to M80 and MOUZ in the competition.

Parivision fell out of the contest after a 2-0 defeat to The MongolZ in Groups, a sadly now expected result for the first Tier 1 trophy winner of 2026.

The roster’s floor has collapsed, and the internal chemistry of the roster has soured into discordance. Coach Dastan ‘dastan’ Aqbaev and rifler Emil ‘nota’ Moskvitin were seen remonstrating even after their win against Lynn Vision.

While Dastan claimed there was “no need to spin some kind of drama out of it” on social media, the coach has openly discussed the idea of replacing players in the lineup. The days of this roster are numbered.

The two were joined on the plane out of Shanghai by B8 and The MongolZ after the conclusion of the Quarter-Finals.

Can anyone stop Falcons at the CS Asia Championships 2026? Let us know on our Discord Server.
For other news, see the details of Insider Gaming’s Showcase!

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/cs-asia-championships-semi-finals-set/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: PlayStation State Of Play June 2026 Showcase—Date & Start Times, How To Watch & Rumored Game Leaks
item.content:encoded:
E3 might be long dead, but the Summer is still the best time for new game announcements and reveals. Who better to lead the charge than PlayStation, as Sony has a State of Play looming on the horizon.

Expect some major news regarding tons of your favorite PlayStation franchises and brands. From release date announcements to world premieres, it’s bound to have it all, and it definitely seems like one worth getting excited for.

You won’t want to miss it, so here’s everything you need to know about the upcoming State of Play showcase!

When Is Sony’s June 2026 State Of Play Showcase?

PlayStation is kicking off the Summer showcase extravaganza, as the latest State of Play is scheduled to premiere on June 2, 2026.

Sony often hosts a State of Play around this time of year, and more often than not, it tends to deliver. It feels like a distant memory when everyone gathered at E3 to see each publisher’s individual showcase, but now they’re all done on an individual basis.

  • More Insider Gaming: Xbox Games Showcase 2026 & Gears of War: E-Day Direct—Date, Start Times & How to Watch

Considering the hype each one garners, it’s difficult to say it was the wrong decision. Sony even announced this State of Play over two weeks in advance, so they must be confident in what they have to show off, right? Regardless, it’s bound to attract millions of views, but when can you tune in for yourself?

What Time Does Sony’s June 2026 State Of Play Start?

It’s been confirmed that the upcoming State of Play will begin at 2 PM PT on June 2, 2026, with other regions experiencing a host of different start times.

For those of you on the West Coast, it’s the perfect midday viewing, whilst for those in Europe or Asia, it’s going to be a late night or very early morning.

Here’s a round-up of key regions for the State of Play start times:

How To Watch Sony’s June 2026 State Of Play

The latest State of Play will be viewable through the usual means on social media, such as YouTube and Twitch.

Many streamers out there are bound to host watchalongs, and some places might even co-stream the event. That said, if you want the direct source to the showcase, you can find it with these links below:

  • YouTube
  • Twitch

How Long Is Sony’s June 2026 State Of Play?

State of Plays vary in duration, and while an exact runtime hasn’t been announced, it’s been confirmed to be over an hour long!

The showcase will even be screened in select Alamo Drafthouse cinemas across the US, with listings suggesting a 90-minute runtime. While that’s likely just to help attendees manage expectations—and the event probably won’t actually run that long—if it comes anywhere close, it could end up being the longest State of Play ever!

  • More Insider Gaming: Summer Game Fest 2026 Showcase—Date & Start Times, How to Watch & How Long it is

It’s clear that there’s going to be a lot showcased at this event, but what exactly can you expect? We’ve got it all broken down below.

What Games Will Appear At Sony’s June 2026 State Of Play?

So far, only one game has had its appearance confirmed for the State of Play, and it’s none other than Marvel’s Wolverine.

The latest adventure in Insomniac’s growing Marvel universe is coming out on September 15, 2026, and Sony is kicking the show off with an in-depth look at new gameplay and details for Wolverine. It’s likely this segment could last upwards of 15 minutes (though that’s pure speculation), leaving what is likely to be another hour of trailers and announcements still to follow.

One game that seems likely to appear is Fairgame$, the multiplayer heist game. Sony recently trademarked the game Break In, and many are speculating that it could be a rebranding for Fairgame$. It seems all too likely given the timing, but nothing has been confirmed.

As for other reveals, the possibilities are truly endless, as Sony has confirmed that we will be hearing from “top studios from around the world.” As always, temper your expectations, but here’s a list of titles that could appear at the showcase, be it updates or announcements:

  • God of War Trilogy Remake
  • Marvel Tokon: Fighting Souls
  • Marathon
  • Intergalactic: The Heretic Prophet
  • Rumored God of War Spinoff featuring Faye
  • Kena: Scars of Kosmora
  • Horizon Hunters Gathering
  • Gran Turismo

All of those aren’t even considering the unknown that’s yet to be revealed. Some studios have long been in the dark; could we finally learn what Media Molecule, Bend Studios or Team Asobi are up to? Time will tell, but fans are hoping for a major showcase, and the cards are in PlayStation’s hands.

It’s time to get your predictions in: what do you think will be revealed during the upcoming State of Play? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server, and don’t forget to tune into the Insider Gaming Showcase on May 28!

For more Insider Gaming content, check out the following:

  • Is Bungie Making Destiny 3 After Destiny 2?
  • All Forza Horizon 6 Welcome to Japan Summer Festival Playlist Events, Challenges & Rewards
  • Everything We Learned About May 2026 Take-Two Earnings Call For GTA 6

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: All Forza Horizon 6 Welcome to Japan Summer Festival Playlist Events, Challenges & Rewards
item.content:encoded:
Forza Horizon 6 is now available worldwide, and the weekly drop of fresh content and challenges has kicked off with the Welcome to Japan Summer Festival Playlist.

Festival Playlists in Forza Horizon 6 are spread across four weeks, each featuring a different season with map changes and challenges, before moving on to the next playlist. This time, the focus is on Welcome to Japan.

For an overview of all the Festival Playlist events and rewards, Insider Gaming has you covered.

All Welcome to Japan Summer Festival Playlist Daily Challenges & Rewards

Daily Challenges in Forza Horizon 6 are, as the name suggests, released daily, but remain available until the end of the current season.

This means that you have a longer stretch of time to complete the first few Daily Challenges, while the final Daily Challenge has a limited window.

The good news is that Daily Challenges can be stacked and don’t have to be completed in the 24 hours after they are first released.

The seven Daily Challenges for the Summer season in the Welcome to Japan Festival Playlist and the respective rewards are outlined in the table below.

All Welcome to Japan Summer Festival Playlist Weekly Challenges & Rewards

Weekly Challenges in the Forza Horizon 6 Festival Playlist are themed around a specific car, and the Welcome to Japan Summer Playlist focuses on the 2022 Toyota GR86.

This means you will need to own the car to tick through the challenges, which must also be completed in order. In total, there are four challenges to work through, with a final reward of Five Festival Points and 25,000 Credits.

Each step of the Weekly Challenge in the Forza Horizon 6 Welcome to Japan Summer Festival Playlist is detailed below.

  1. Own and Drive the 2022 Toyota GR86.
  2. Earn 3 Stars at Speed Zones in the 2022 Toyota GR86.
  3. Earn 6 Speed Skills in the 2022 Toyota GR86
  4. Win the Hakone Nanamagari Touge Race in the 2022 Toyota GR86.

All Welcome to Japan Summer Festival Playlist Events & Rewards

The table below highlights all other Seasonal events and challenges in the Welcome to Japan Summer Festival Playlist, and all the available rewards.

What do you make of these rewards? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

Don’t forget to tune into the Insider Gaming Showcase on May 28, featuring 55 total games, including 12 World Premieres.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

item.title: Lastest Riot Vanguard Update Can Brick Your Hardware (If You’re a Cheater)
item.content:encoded:
Riot Games‘ kernel-level anti-cheat, Vanguard, has received an update that is allegedly altering system firmware to remove the ability of the user to access certain hardware associated with cheating.

Riot Games quoted one post discussing the anti-cheat, replying “congrats to the owners of a brand new $6k paperweight.” But how exactly does Vanguard’s new system make “paperweights” out of hardware?

How Riot’s new Vanguard update works

The update targets DMA (Direct Memory Access) cheating hardware, which works by circumventing kernel-level anti-cheat by directly accessing the game’s memory from a device that is not a part of the user’s system.

For that to work, the system needs to disguise that it is talking to another piece of hardware by making the cheat run on the secondary hardware. Because the cheats are not running on the original system, the cheats cannot be detected by software on that system.

Therefore, instead of targeting an undetectable system, Riot’s new Vanguard update targets NVMe and SATA drive firmware, which enables the DMA cheating devices, modifying files upon cheating detection to break how the firmware works, and preventing the DMA from communicating with the original system.

The ability to detect the firmware necessary for the cheats comes after collaboration between Riot and various motherboard manufacturers such as MSI, ASRock, and ASUS.

Upon detection, users are shown a red warning screen, which has various messages poking fun at cheaters. Some messages shared on social media include:

“Expectations incorrectly configured. Cheat stated of [UNDETECTED] is incompatiable with current account state of [BANNED]. If the issues persists, consider contacting your local cheat developer for a refund.”

and

This is a manual ban receipt for the current account, which has been banned for cheating manually. While the outcome is identical to an automatic van, this was only a manual ban. Cop level has been logged for tourbleshooting.”

The user is then taken to an IOMMU (I/O Memory Management Unit) warning, at which point the DMA and the system will effectively be prevented from communicating with each other, killing the usability of the DMA for that system.

Do you think Anti-Cheat should have that level of access to your system? Let us know on Insider Gaming’s Discord


For other news, see the details of Insider Gaming’s Showcase

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/lastest-riot-vanguard-update-can-brick-your-hardware-if-youre-a-cheater/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Is Bungie Making Destiny 3 After Destiny 2?
item.content:encoded:
It’s official: Destiny 2 is receiving its final expansion, Monument of Triumph, on June 9, 2026. After that, there will be no more content updates coming to the game, marking the end of an era for millions of fans across the world.

Of course, Destiny 2 wasn’t the beginning, with the story starting all the way back in 2014 with the original Destiny. Considering that it’s a franchise, and the second game is coming to a close, it’s natural to expect a third entry to follow up shortly afterwards, especially when you consider all of the loose ends and plot points still left to explore!

However, the reality of the situation is something else entirely. Here’s whether Bungie is developing Destiny 3, and what the future of Destiny looks like.

Is Destiny 3 In Development?

Despite the end of Destiny 2, it does not appear that Destiny 3 is in active production.

That news comes from Jason Schreier, who revealed in a Bloomberg report that Destiny 3 is not actively being worked on by the team over at Bungie. It’s led a lot to question why the sequel wasn’t immediately prioritised after the conclusion of The Final Shape, the expansion that put an end to the Light and Dark saga.

  • More Insider Gaming: Is Destiny 2 Shutting Down? Final Destiny 2 Update Details

Well, as tends to often be the case in the industry, it all boils down to money. Destiny 3 would cost a fortune to develop, and it seems like Sony is sceptical about greenlighting the sequel amid challenging market conditions. So, don’t expect a Destiny 3 announcement at the upcoming State of Play, as it seems all but impossible.

What Is Bungie Working On Next?

That begs the question: if Bungie isn’t working on a Destiny 2 follow-up, what are they doing instead?

Well, priorities have been shifted internally towards Marathon, with many of the Destiny 2 team having been moved to work on the extraction shooter instead. Despite poor sales and player numbers, Bungie seems determined to stick by its latest release, at least for the near future.

Outside of Marathon, it’s been reported that staff are looking to pitch new projects, but nothing has been greenlit just yet. In fact, Bungie is planning for a batch of major layoffs, with numbers currently unclear. A dwindling team of developers continues to bode bad news for the future of the company, especially after they cost Sony a $765 million loss.

What’s Next For Destiny?

The future of Destiny has never looked so uncertain, and it’s impossible to definitively say what’s next for the franchise.

Everyone would have banked on Destiny 3 being the obvious future for Bungie, but with that seemingly on the ropes, it looks like we might have to wait years for more Destiny content.

  • More Insider Gaming: Everything We Learned About May 2026 Take-Two Earnings Call For GTA 6

Destiny 2 will remain playable with the final Monument of Triumphs expansion, and there’s always Destiny: Rising, the mobile game, if you’re desperate for a hit. However, when it comes to the Destiny experience that we’ve all grown to love, it’s difficult to say when something new will come our way.

Some of the projects being pitched at Bungie by staff members are Destiny adjacent, but even they’re not being given the green light. Until Bungie shares more about its upcoming slate of games, all we have is hope that Destiny will return sooner rather than later.

How do you feel about the future of Destiny? Share your best Destiny 2 memories in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server, and don’t forget to tune into the Insider Gaming Showcase on May 28!

For more Insider Gaming content, check out the following:

  • Has GTA 6’s Release Date Been Delayed Again? (May 2026)
  • Xbox Games Showcase 2026 & Gears of War: E-Day Direct—Date, Start Times & How to Watch
  • Summer Game Fest 2026 Showcase—Date & Start Times, How to Watch & How Long it is

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: Forza Horizon 6 Festival Playlist: Ohtani Treasure Hunt Location & Reward
item.content:encoded:
After an early access period, Forza Horizon 6 has been released to the masses, and the first Festival Playlist has been launched with Welcome to Japan, providing a Treasure Hunt to complete in Ohtani.

The first seasonal playlist of Forza Horizon 6 kicked off on May 28 with Summer, marking the start of a four-week period of new content drops and seasonal changes in the Welcome to Japan Playlist.

If you want to hunt down the new treasure chest in Forza Horizon 6, Insider Gaming has the details you need.

Where to Find The Ohtani Treasure Hunt Chest in Forza Horizon 6

The Ohtani Treasure Hunt Chest in the Forza Horizon 6 Welcome to Japan Summer Festival Playlist is found in the area around the Kinkaku-Ji Temple, highlighted on the map above.

There is no cutscene or anything that triggers, like when you find a Treasure Car or locate a Barn Find, and, instead, just smash through the Chest, as you do for the Mascots and Horizon XP Boards spread across the map.

  • More Insider Gaming: All Forza Horizon 6 Wheelspin Exclusive Cars, Listed

If you have already visted the location, you can fast travel straight there. If not, work your way through the off-road route nearby, and when you enter the Temple’s area, turn left from the entrance and follow the road around the pond.

Another trick is to check the map for nearby players, as it’s likely that there are plenty of people hanging out by the location.

What Do You Get From The Treasure Hunt Chest?

Hunting down the Treasure Chest in the Ohtani region during the Summer season of the Forza Horizon 6 Welcome to Japan playlist is certainly worthwhile, with the main reward being a hefty chunk of 100,000 Credits.

Credits are the lifeblood of Forza Horizon 6, used for everything from buying new cars and new houses, to upgrading your favorite vehicle and customization.

  • More Insider Gaming: What is The Fastest Car in Forza Horizon 6?

While it doesn’t take too long to boast millions of Credits in Forza Horizon 6, it’s always worth making sure you have as much as possible, particularly with the regular content drops and new offerings.

The last thing you want is to have something new added to the game that you’re desperate for, only to discover you’re short on the Credits needed.

Are you enjoying the Welcome to Japan Festival Playlist in Forza Horizon 6? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

Don’t forget to tune into the Insider Gaming Showcase on May 28, featuring 55 total games, including 12 World Premieres.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Is Destiny 2 Shutting Down? Final Destiny 2 Update Details
item.content:encoded:
The Destiny banner has been flying since 2014, with the franchise going through plenty of highs and lows across the years. For every The Final Shape, there was a Curse of Osiris, but there’s no denying that there’s just no other game out there quite like Destiny.

Well, that might hit especially hard now, as Bungie has recently announced its final live-service content update for Destiny 2, and it has a whole crowd of fans asking the same question: Is Destiny 2 shutting down?

It’d be the end of an incredible saga, but the future does seem set in stone. Here are all the details regarding the final expansion for Destiny 2, and whether the game is being shut down for good.

What Is Destiny 2’s Final Expansion?

Destiny 2’s final expansion is titled Monument of Triumph, and it’s set to be a celebration of everything players have loved about the franchise over the years.

Typically, Moments of Triumph would arrive annually and celebrate highlights from Destiny 2’s most recent chapter, but now it’s being elevated into its own expansion. The goal is to make it a welcoming place for players to return to, and there’s a ton of content arriving, including:

  • Newly refreshed Director returns to the center of activities
  • Pantheon 2.0 arrives as a permanent addition with a fresh slate of bosses
  • All Raid and Dungeon loot has been revisited and updated
  • Destinations have been refreshed with Distortions and updated loot
  • A host of new abilities and reworks for some specific ones
  • The Portal will be completely overhauled
  • New crucible game modes
  • An updated rewards pass tied to the Monument of Triumph
  • Sparrow Racing League returns as a permanent addition

Of course, with this being the final Destiny 2 expansion, seasonal events will be retired. Weapon rewards from the likes of The Dawning and Guardian Games will be made available through engrams via a Monument of Triumph vendor.

  • More Insider Gaming: Bungie Planning For Major Layoffs as Destiny 2 Stops Development

It’s looking to be a real trip down memory lane, and it will hopefully be the most comprehensive Destiny 2 experience available. Of course, with no more left in the game’s sights, does this mean that Destiny 2 is shutting down?

Is Destiny 2 Shutting Down?

No, Destiny 2 will not be shutting down, and Bungie will ensure that the game remains fully playable.

Active development on the game will be concluding, but that doesn’t mean that Bungie is throwing Destiny 2 away. A huge part of the Monument of Triumph expansion is to ensure that the game remains a welcoming place for players to return to, even if new content is no longer on the horizon.

  • More Insider Gaming: How To Get The Rascal Grenade Launcher In ARC Raiders—Blueprint Location

Even though the game may not be shutting down for good, it’s truly a sad time for any Destiny fan out there. Considering the fact that it’s been in people’s lives for upwards of 13 years, to see it come to an end is a genuinely bittersweet feeling. It will always be there to revisit, but it seems like the journey across the stars is coming to an end.

Are you sad to see Destiny 2 sunset? Share your best Destiny 2 memories in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server, and don’t forget to tune into the Insider Gaming Showcase on May 28!

For more Insider Gaming content, check out the following:

  • Has GTA 6’s Release Date Been Delayed Again? (May 2026)
  • Xbox Games Showcase 2026 & Gears of War: E-Day Direct—Date, Start Times & How to Watch
  • Summer Game Fest 2026 Showcase—Date & Start Times, How to Watch & How Long it is

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

item.title: Everything We Learned About May 2026 Take-Two Earnings Call For GTA 6
item.content:encoded:
All eyes in 2026 continue to be firmly set on the long-awaited release of GTA 6, and plenty of attention was paid to the May 2026 Take-Two Earnings call, where there was lots to learn.

GTA 6 is arguably the most anticipated game of all time, with over a decade since the release of the last game in the Grand Theft Auto franchise, GTA 5, and eight years since Rockstar Games‘ last major published title, Red Dead Redemption 2.

If you’re looking to see the highlights of what we learned from the Take-Two earnings call, Insider Gaming has the details.

Everything We Learned From Take-Two 2026 Earnings Call

GTA 6 Release Date Unchanged

The news that will undoubtedly have led to huge sighs of relief from the community was confirmation that the release date of GTA 6 has not changed, ruling out another delay.

GTA 6 has been hit with two delays thus far, first pushing the game back to May 26, 2026, and then sliding the game back further on the calendar by adjusting the release date to November 19, 2026.

Speculation of another delay has been persistent ever since, particularly with Rockstar Games not providing any further updates, but the Take-Two earnings call confirmed there has been no change.

  • Read More: Take-Two Confirms GTA 6 Release Date… Hasn’t Changed

Take-Two Forecasts Mammoth $8 Billion Revenue

Unsurprisingly, the earnings call placed a lot of focus on exactly that, and forecasts for the next fiscal year showed mammoth expectations, of course pushed by the launch of GTA 6.

Take-Two forecasts a whopping $8 billion in revenue in the 2026 Fiscal Year, heavily pushed by the long-awaited release date of GTA 6.

Those totals will include all sales figures for GTA 6, alongside any other additional purchases available, like Shark Cards for GTA Online.

  • Read More: Take-Two Forecasts $8 Billion in Revenue In Its Next Fiscal Year Thanks to GTA 6

No Price Reveal For GTA 6

One thing we didn’t learn from the Take-Two earnings call about GTA 6 was the price of the title, amid continued rumors that the price point could be as much as $100.

No comments were made surrounding the pricing structure, and whether the base version of the game would set new heights for gaming titles, and, instead, those announcements will be left to Rockstar Games themselves.

Pre-order details are expected to go live very soon, along with a long-awaited third trailer, which could finally put the questions around pricing to bed.

  • Read More: GTA 6 Price Won’t Be Revealed in Take-Two Earnings Call

What was your big takeaway from the Take-Two Earnings Call? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

Don’t forget to tune into the Insider Gaming Showcase on May 28, featuring 55 total games, including 12 World Premieres.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: IEM Cologne 2026 Major Stickers Added, but Without Capsules; donk Sticker Over $1,000
item.content:encoded:
Valve has released the Stickers for the IEM Cologne 2026 Major, but without the classic stickers capsules that Counter-Strike players are used to.

Players will now purchase stickers directly through the ‘2026 Cologne Major Shop,’ taking Counter-Strike another step away from the randomized case and capsule opening that has been the backbone of its financial model for over a decade.

Sticker demands over $1,000 in the new system

Instead of capsules, players will now have to buy tokens (minimum 100, which is $0.99 USD) in order to then redeem items.

The price of the stickers is variable, depending on demand. Therefore, the most highly sought-after stickers will end up being the most expensive.

At the time of writing, the most desired sticker is the donk gold sticker, demanding 122,972 tokens, or an eyewatering $1,217.42.

When explaining the decision to directly offer stickers, Valve explained, “We’ve heard feedback that players might prefer to purchase stickers directly. Additionally, players in certain regions cannot purchase sticker capsules. So we’re exploring alternatives that allow all players to participate.

The list of countries where case opening is either banned or circumvented through the X-ray mechanic has grown, with the Netherlands, Germany, and Belgium banning or restricting the practice in recent months.

Valve is also under significant legal pressure surrounding cases in the United States, facing lawsuits in New York and Washington State that seek to include loot boxes under existing gambling legislation.

The recent Terminals added to the game have also removed the traditional loot box element, instead prompting players to directly buy the skins they are offered.

Therefore, Stickers are simply yet another shift away from Counter-Strike’s randomized model as pressure grows around the nature of loot boxes and mystery capsules.

Souvenirs can now be traded up after the Souvenir-O-Matic was introduced

In another shift, Valve announced that Souvenir skins would also shift. With players now offering their skins to the Souvenir-O-Matic, which will convert the skin to a Souvenir skin representing a completed match.

The process also costs tokens, with the rate depending on the demand for the gold team stickers, the autograph stickers involved, and the rarity of the skin.

The change means that Souvenir skins are closer to regular skins in the game files, meaning Souvenir skins can now be traded up for non-Souvenir skins.

Previously, low-tier Souvenir skins were essentially worthless as they were a dead end; they could not be traded up, therefore, they would stay bad forever, making them some of the cheapest items in the game.

Now players can trade up 10 of those skins for a higher-tier item in the collection of the traded-up skins, giving low-tier souvenirs at least some value.

Do you prefer the old system or like the new one? Let us know on Insider Gaming’s Discord!
For other news, see the details of Insider Gaming’s Showcase!

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.
